## Highlights

**Standalone windows** — the 推演 ("how to reply") drilldown and the diagnostics Field Log are now their own movable OS windows (#28, #29).

**Reliable, streaming analysis** — the timeline and post-meeting action items stream into the UI as they generate. Structured-output calls are resilient (fixes Groq `gpt-oss` `json_validate_failed`), with rich error logging in the Field Log window.

**Grounded in real negotiation theory** — analysis and replies are anchored in Harvard Principled Negotiation: interests vs positions, BATNA, ZOPA, objective criteria, and options for mutual gain — plus a per-deal setup (your BATNA / target / bottom line) that's fed into every prompt to reason about leverage and the ZOPA.

**Sharper findings** — a single moment can match several evaluations and cite multiple quotes (e.g. both sides of a contradiction). Prompts were redesigned to work at the level of meaningful exchanges and classify accurately (a question isn't a claim; a legitimate constraint isn't deception). The reply coach always replies from *your* side.

**Settings & UX** — refreshed model list + custom-model input, pricing for the new models, context dialogs before analysis (re-analyze / ingest / live), and many fixes.
